raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I made a purchase from XXXX totaling {$1500.00} USD using my American Express card. The order was supposed to arrive by XX/XX/year>, but I never received the package. The USPS tracking information shows three separate delivery scans within a 30-minute window for the same tracking number, which makes no sense and strongly suggests a delivery or scanning error. 

I filed a dispute with American Express on XX/XX/year>, but it was denied on XX/XX/year> without a formal investigation. I submitted a reappeal on XX/XX/year>, which was again denied the same day within only a few hours. No evidence of a proper investigation was provided, and no photo proof or recipient signature exists to confirm delivery. 

XXXX provided minimal assistance and refused to help further, stating they would not assist because I had initiated a chargeback. As a result, I am left without the product or refund for {$1500.00}. 

I am requesting that the CFPB require American Express to reopen this dispute and conduct a proper investigation under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1666 ) and ensure XXXX provides verifiable delivery proof or a full refund.
